case.title: LAIKIND v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF NEW YORK

opinion.h1: LAIKIND v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF NEW YORK, 397 U.S. 148 (1970)
opinion.h4: LAIKIND v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF NEW YORK APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK No. 68. Decided March 2, 1970
opinion.p: Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, pro se, Samuel A. Hirshowitz, First Assistant Attorney General, and Warren M. Goidel, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a final judgment.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that further consideration of the question of jurisdiction should be postponed to the hearing of the case on the merits.
